figures.caption: Figure 2: Trajectory-Persistent Attack Experiment: Mitigation and Reward-Gap Results. (D) Mitigation effect: adversarial fine-tuning (PGD-10 on t = 0) reduces Ak substantially at all steps (before: solid; after: dashed). (E) Perturbation budget sensitivity before and after mitigation; the hardened model maintains lower error across the full ε range. (F) Absolute cumulative reward (clean vs. perturbed) as a function of planning horizon H; the reward gap at H = 30 is 0.000892±0.000057 — small in absolute terms, confirming the GRU proxy operates in a regime of moderate (not catastrophic) reward damage; the world model gap remains consistently larger than the single-step gap. All error bands show ±1 SE.

Table 3: Claim–evidence map. Confidence levels: High = strong proof-of-concept evidence, direction consistent with theory; Moderate = pilot evidence, consistent with prior literature; Low–Moderate = partial empirical signal with theoretical support; Speculative = open hypothesis, no direct evidence.

Figure 1: Trajectory-Persistent Adversarial Attack Experiment (V3 Results): Core Results. (A) Mean latent-state error (ℓ2) over K = 30 rollout steps following a single adversarial perturbation at t = 0 (ε = 0.05, N = 200 trials, GRU-based RSSM). The world model (WM, blue) amplifies the perturbation at step 1 (A1 = 2.26×) before GRU contraction attenuates it; the single-step baseline (SS, orange) shows no state-mediated amplification. (B) Architecture comparison: trajectory amplification ratio Ak on a log scale for the deterministic GRU world model vs. a stochastic RSSM proxy (posterior at t = 0, prior rollout thereafter). The RSSM proxy shows lower initial amplification (A1 = 0.65×) and slower decay, confirming architecture-dependence. (C) Real DreamerV3 checkpoint probe (seed 0): per-metric bar chart showing A1, normalised latent error E1, and action drift ∥∆a1∥. Non-zero coupling confirms representational perturbations propagate into policy outputs. Panels (D)–(F) (mitigation effect, budget sensitivity, reward gap) appear in Figure 2 in the appendix. All error bands show ±1 SE.
Table 1: Attacker Capability Taxonomy for World Model Systems
